prose:
Frontier-orbitals task run on aniline using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (chemkit tier 'standard', density-fit), computed with PySCF in the gas phase (no implicit solvent), charge 0, multiplicity 1 (closed-shell RKS). Geometry: user-supplied aniline.xyz (14 atoms) used as-is (single-point; not re-optimized at this level). SCF converged in 9 cycles. Engine integrity verdict: status 'ok', trustworthy = true. No warnings were reported (the warnings array was empty — clean run).

Headline result: HOMO-LUMO gap = 5.3048177 eV (Koopmans-style orbital-energy difference, not a TD-DFT excitation energy). HOMO = -5.553772 eV, LUMO = -0.2489544 eV. Additional orbitals: HOMO-2 = -8.4041702 eV, HOMO-1 = -6.8461949 eV, LUMO+1 = +0.30750756 eV, LUMO+2 = +0.77617648 eV. Koopmans descriptors: vertical IP = 5.553772 eV, vertical EA = 0.2489544 eV, electronegativity = 2.9013632 eV, chemical hardness = 2.6524088 eV, chemical softness = 0.37701579 eV^-1, electrophilicity index = 1.5868422 eV. Total electronic energy = -287.71578 Hartree (= -7829.1453 eV = -180544.38 kcal/mol); energy zero = electronic energy (bare nuclei + electrons).

Reproducibility: cli.py frontier --method dft --functional b3lyp --basis def2-tzvp --tier standard .../aniline/aniline.xyz. No experimental/literature comparison is offered.
